quote.author: Russian proverb ("доверяй, но проверяй"), popularised in English by Ronald Reagan during the 1987 INF Treaty negotiations

macro.title: What It Means for VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

reads.label: VWRA & bonds
reads.text: squeezed by a hawkish Fed and an idiosyncratic AI selloff at once; energy strength offers no equity offset since it's stagflationary, not a demand signal.

reads.label: Gold
reads.text: its worst week in six months — the oil-inflation scare is doing more to it than the war, extending the decoupling this desk flagged in No. 45.

reads.label: Bitcoin
reads.text: fell with risk-off equities, the high-beta liquidity proxy this desk has always read it as, not a hedge.

stories.headline: The Odyssey's tracking range resolves into the biggest opening of Nolan's career, and the highest Tomatometer score of it too
stories.dek: Last issue's "$85-120M, no actual number yet" is this issue's real number — and it landed at the top of the range, not the middle.

stories.headline: A nearby rocky world's atmosphere was there in 2024, gone in 2025, and back again — and an amateur beats the geologists to a 390-million-year-old crater
stories.dek: Both stories share the same discipline: don't call an anomaly permanent, or a shape a coincidence, until you've checked it more than once.

stories.headline: Iran formally suspends its ceasefire commitments, on the war's bloodiest and longest week yet
stories.dek: The Islamabad MoU passed its own halfway point this week. Tehran's answer was to say, plainly, that it no longer considers itself bound by it.

stories.headline: Changi's route wishlist finally gets a timeline, as the Auditor-General flags lapses across several agencies
stories.dek: A decade-out capacity plan gets some honest scheduling detail, in the same week a routine annual report does its quiet job of keeping everyone else honest.

stories.headline: Four projects, 1,679 units — June's zero-launch drought breaks wide, and a price index quietly splits by postcode
stories.dek: The rebound spans everything from Turf City to Jurong Lake — but Q2's flash numbers show prime and mass-market prices no longer moving together.

stories.headline: DeepSeek's legacy API gets a hard, dateable deadline — and Megaspeed's whitelist case finally has real texture
stories.dek: Two compliance stories this fortnight are both graduating from abstraction to specifics — a 404 error with a date attached, and a named company's actual defence.

stories.headline: Samsung's Galaxy Glasses leak as screenless and audio-first, four days before Unpacked says so officially
stories.dek: A deliberate bet against a display, mirroring Meta's own playbook — and a very different pair of glasses shipping the same season for a very different buyer.

stories.headline: A $10 leak sensor and a rumoured Apple hub sit at opposite ends of the same migration
stories.dek: Apple Home's promised overhaul stops being vapourware this fortnight — a public beta is out and a ship date is finally attached.

stories.headline: A diamond diaphragm and a 40th-anniversary speaker lead a strong week for premium audio, as two watch auctions edge toward an answer
stories.dek: The Rainbow Daytona's first tranche closes tomorrow — the last of three live sales this desk has held open two issues running.

stories.headline: California moves to certify "non-ultraprocessed" food, as the category doing the alarming turns out to be poorly defined itself
stories.dek: A policy delta on the ultra-processed-food story No. 45 introduced — and a genuine scientific complaint about the label at the centre of it.

stories.kicker: The Macro Desk · Market Explanation, Not Advice
stories.headline: A hawkish Fed and an oil shock pull the same portfolio from two directions at once, with an AI-valuation wobble layered on top
stories.dek: Gold's decoupling from war risk (No. 45) keeps going — but this week it's an oil-driven inflation scare doing more to it than the war itself.

stories.headline: Hinge's own founder builds the thing the matchmaker surge (No. 45) was asking for
stories.dek: A voice-first, no-swipe-feed matchmaking startup, backed by the very company whose engagement numbers are sliding.

stories.headline: A second, independent ageing-muscle gene switch turns up — before the first one (No. 45) has even settled in
stories.dek: General guidance, not medical advice — a broccoli-derived compound is the early, honestly-flagged hook, not a prescription.

stories.headline: A Slipped Cabin, a Fare Sale, and a Tunnel That's Actually Finished
stories.dek: No single transformative route this fortnight — a delayed product launch, a genuinely useful sale, and one real piece of underground concrete poured on schedule.

stories.headline: Three of Japan's biggest summer matsuri land on the same weekend, resolving the "following week" note this desk held open last issue
stories.dek: BIGBANG's Singapore silence, meanwhile, reaches a tenth straight issue — sharpened by its Korea shows selling out so fast that extra tickets had to open.

stories.headline: The Long Look
stories.dek: A ceasefire that finally says "suspended." A planet whose atmosphere vanished for a year before reappearing. A watch valley that won't call an instrument accurate until it has failed to prove itself wrong for two straight weeks. This issue is full of things that only tell the truth to whoever is patient enough to keep checking.
